details.title: Time With Her
details.description:
Time With Her
The dimension of time has for all of us seemed to warp and bend at different occasions. When you are in the presence of something or someone you love, all of a sudden time can seem to have passed by so quickly, while also seeming to not have passed by at all. In this state, what appeared to be obstacles just moments before, now all of a sudden look like nothing at all.
Take this short journey to remind yourself of that timeless state.

Controls
A: Move left
D: Move right
Spacebar: Jump
E: Interact
R: Restart level
Volume slider exists in the top left corner
Used assets
DOTween (HOTween v2) --- Demigiant (Unity tools)
Input Prompts Pixel 16x --- Kenney (Input prompt sprites)
Sweet Thoughts --- Ruud (Music)
FREE Casual Game SFX Pack --- Dustyroom (SoundFX)
FREE CASUAL PACK SFX --- mk.matheusklein (SoundFX)
Footsteps Mini Sound Pack --- MGWSoundDesign (Footstep sounds)
BoldPixels --- YuxiPixels (Font)

game.short_text: Every life.. Every choice counts.. in the very last second.
game.title: The Last Second

details.title: The Last Second
details.description:
NOTE: Scroll down to download the PC version for a better experience 🖥️
🧭 Overview
The Last Second is a Unity 20th Anniversary Game Jam entry built around the “Timeless” theme — and developed entirely using the official Unity 20th Anniversary Assets.
 
You play as a scientist trapped in a collapsing temporal facility — where both humans and AI are moments away from destruction. Time has stopped for the world, but not for you. You have one final second to make impossible choices.
 
Will you save the humans who created the technology? The robots who evolved beyond it? Or just yourself — and the cube that holds infinity?
 
 🏆 Eligible for the “Most Creative Use of Anniversary Assets” category.
 
⚙️ Gameplay
  Explore a fractured lab frozen in time.
   Solve environmental puzzles to unlock safety pods for humans and robots.
   Use UNITY Infinity Cube to extend your remaining time.
   Make irreversible choices — each rescue shapes the story and determines one of 8 unique endings:
   Only the player survives.
   Player saves only one.
   Player saves all 3 robots.
   Player saves all 3 humans.
   Player saves everyone.
   No one survives.
   Player saves 3 humans and himself.
   Player saves himself and the Unity Infinity Cube.
🎮 Controls
ActionKeyMove
WASD / Arrow Keys
Interact / Pick
E
Jump
Space
Sprint
Shift
StartEnter
💡 Features
  Real-time countdown mechanic with slow-motion time effects
   Eight distinct branching endings
   Stylized sci-fi environment (built entirely using Unity 20th Anniversary Assets)
   Cinematic storytelling through camera and lighting
   Emotional soundtrack matching each ending outcome
  
🧰 Built Using
 🧩 Unity 20th Anniversary Assets Pack Every character, prop, and environment in The Last Second has been crafted using the Anniversary collection. This project showcases how narrative and atmosphere can be built creatively from shared assets, honoring Unity’s 20 years of empowering creators.
 
👨‍💻 Team
Developer & Designer: Abdul Razaq (Tecshield) Engine: Unity 6.2 LTS Game Jam Theme: Timeless Category: Most Creative Use of Anniversary Assets Development Time: 48 Hours


Every decision you make echoes through time. How will you spend your last second?
💾 Download & Install Instructions (Windows PC)
  Click the “Download” button on this page to get the game file.
   File name: TheLastSecond_Windows.zip
     Extract the ZIP file to any folder on your PC.
   Right-click → “Extract All…” (or use WinRAR/7-Zip).
     Inside the extracted folder, locate the game executable:
   TheLastSecond.exe
     Double-click TheLastSecond.exe to start the game.
   No installation required — it runs directly from the folder.
     If Windows shows a security prompt (“Windows protected your PC”), click More info → Run anyway — this happens because it’s an unsigned indie build.
   Enjoy the game!
   Recommended resolution: 1920×1080 (Full HD)
   Engine: Unity 6 (2025 build)
   Platform: Windows 10 or later
   Controls: Keyboard + Mouse (Controller optional)
    
⚙️ Troubleshooting
  If the game doesn’t start, ensure:
   Your antivirus isn’t blocking the .exe file.
   You’ve fully extracted the ZIP (don’t run it from inside WinRAR).
   Your GPU drivers and DirectX are up to date.
     For the best experience, play in fullscreen mode and close background apps.

moreInfo.Author: Abdul Razaq Ali
moreInfo.Tags: 20thanniversary, Global Game Jam, Indie, Meaningful Choices, My First Game Jam, Narrative, Sci-fi, Singleplayer, timeless, Unity

game.title: PENDULUM
game.short_text: A game about a pendulum that leaves the confines of its clock
Timeless Memories
game.title: Who are you again?

details.title: Who are you again?
details.description:
Who are you again? is an exploration and puzzle game where you step into the father’s memories through his photographs. Each photo transforms into a 3D scene to explore, filled with interactive elements and puzzles that guide you from one memory to the next.
As you piece together fragments of his past, you move through moments where time no longer follows a clear path — a world where days, years, and faces blur together. In this space between memory and reality, you uncover the bond between a father and his son, and how love endures even when time itself is lost.
Immerse yourself in a journey of nostalgia and discovery, uncovering the fleeting moments that shaped a life. Every scene is carefully crafted to evoke the mood and atmosphere of a memory, encouraging reflection, curiosity, and emotional engagement.
 
Gameplay
  Explore small, detailed 3D environments based on photographs.
   Solve environmental puzzles and uncover fragments of the father’s  story.
   Interact with symbolic objects to transition between memories.
   Experience a narrative told through visuals and exploration rather than dialogue.
Controls
Movement: WASD  Interact: E  Pause: Esc
Credits
Programmer : paxcuProgrammer : Andrés MayoProgrammer : Egoitz ZubeldiaDesigner: NelarArtist: CarmuxArtist: nenepesaurio
Music and SFX:
Music by Blue Dot Sessions & ReederOpen book by manuelaurreaf -- https://freesound.org/s/490111/ -- License: Attribution 4.0Footsteps on Dirt by IsmailBulbulia -- https://freesound.org/s/765520/ -- License: Attribution NonCommercial 4.0Waterfall, Small, A.wav by InspectorJ -- https://freesound.org/s/365915/ -- License: Attribution 4.0About
Created for: Unity 20th Anniversary Game Jam


Engine: Unity
Genre: Exploration, Puzzle 
Perspective: 3D, fixed camera

"Who Are You Again?" was built as part of the Unity 20th Anniversary Game Jam, created around the theme Timeless. It features custom modifications of existing assets, tailored to fit the tone and atmosphere of each memory. Every puzzle, interaction, and scene is designed to guide players through a delicate, poetic exploration of life, memory, and moments that transcend time.

game.short_text: Dodge, drift, and defy time itself in a chaotic physics loop that never ends — unless you do.
game.title: When Time Forgot To End

details.title: When Time Forgot To End
details.description:
About
"When Time Forgot to End" is a chaotic, physics-driven survival experience set in a world where time has stopped but motion hasn’t.
 You drive through a collapsing lab where every collision, every bounce, and every second adds to your eternity. The laws of physics are broken, and you’re caught inside the moment that refuses to finish.
 
Your only goal? Break the cycle before you become part of it.
Game Lore
The experiment was meant to end time itself. But the lab’s Flux Capacitor malfunctioned, and the clock never stopped.
 
Time fractured. Physics lost control.
 
The machines kept running.Energy kept moving. Collisions never settled.
 
You’re trapped inside the failure where motion loops forever and never finds its effect.
Break the cycle. Escape the lab. Or become another machine caught in motion... In a world where "Time Forgot To End".


Dev Summary & Theme Fit


"When Time Forgot to End" was my second idea for the jam. The first prototype didn’t feel as fun or close to what I had imagined, so I pivoted quickly to a simpler and more expressive concept: What if time stopped, but physics didn’t?
 
The theme “Timeless” inspired me to build a world stuck in one infinite moment, a chaotic lab where motion never ends. I used the Robot Lab (Unity 20th Anniversary Pack) as the foundation, but completely retextured and rematerialized it to match my poppy, arcade-style vision. I baked a basic lightmap to highlight the environment’s depth and energy.
 
From the U1.2 Starter Assets, I used the car and character meshes, and even turned a smooth cube into a power-up, repurposing assets creatively to fit the gameplay loop.
 
Due to time constraints, I went with a minimalist UI, focusing on clarity and efficiency. I also added a short tutorial/FTUE to help players quickly understand the mechanics, something I’ve learned is crucial for consistent feedback and player enjoyment.
 
Late in development,  I recognized that replayability was lacking, so I implemented basic difficulty settings to allow players to increase the challenge and enjoy the game more consistently.
 
The game still has a few rough edges, no player boundaries, and a few physics quirks I wish I could’ve polished more. If time had allowed, I’d have loved to add more visual depth and effects to bring the chaos of the world to life.
 
Even so, I’m proud of how this project captures the essence of Timeless, a world where motion, energy, and chaos outlive time itself

game.short_text: A handmade collage toy / idle game.
game.title: Momentia

details.title: Momentia
details.description:
A handmade collage toy / idle game.
Everything you see was drawn on paper, cut out with scissors, and scanned - every plant, stone, and scrap. Then reassembled as a layered 3D diorama.
It’s part idle game, part collage machine, part memory of rainy afternoons making things by hand. You grow strange alien flora on a forgotten planet. Each plant slowly produces energy, letting you plant more and watch the scene come alive.
There’s no rush. The world unfolds at its own pace - as if time doesn’t really matter here.




About the theme - “Timeless”
Collages are timeless.

They existed before screens and will outlast them - paper, glue, scissors, patience. Momentia takes that simple act of making things and stretches it into an idle loop. You build something that keeps growing even when you look away.
It’s a little reminder that small, handmade moments can last forever.




How to Play


Select flora → place with E / Spacebar.
Move →  WASD / ArrowsEach plant produces energy automatically over time.
Use that energy to unlock new species and expand your little ecosystem.
Leave it running. Come back later. 
That’s it. It’s a small thing. Let it grow.

moreInfo.Author: Lost Cyclops
moreInfo.Tags: Cats, Cozy, Economy, Hand-drawn, Idle, Incremental, Indie, Relaxing, Sandbox, upgrades

game.short_text: A timeless classic reborn, where every second is survival! How long can you outrun eternity?
game.title: DeltaTime

details.title: DeltaTime
details.description:
ΔTIME
The timeless arcade classic reborn in neon. Made for the UNITY 20th Anniversary Game Jam.
Drift through the glowing void in this future-retro re-imagining of the classic Asteroids.
 You begin with thirty seconds, but the clock is always ticking down. Every asteroid you shatter and every enemy you destroy gives you precious fragments of time. But take a hit, and your seconds are stolen away.
Syncing with the pulse of an electronic beat, the void itself comes alive. Enemies spawn with the rhythm, lights flare to the bass, and every moment demands precision and flow.
Fight to stay alive, ride the rhythm, and chase the infinite. How long can you remain timeless?
ControlsThrust [W]Aim / Direction [Mouse]Fire Weapon [Left Mouse-Click]

ΔTIME - OST - Main Theme

moreInfo.Tags: Arcade, Asteroids, Music, Neon, Space, Unity

game.short_text: An atmospheric, cultish strategy with Goddess that stops the time
game.title: La Maestra

details.title: La Maestra
details.description:
FOR BEST EXPERIENCE DOWNLOAD WINDOWS VERSION!
Post Jam version released! Check bottom for Roadmap (Changelog)


La Maestra is a real-time cult management game created for the Unity 20th Anniversary Game Jam in just 48 hours. All graphics, models, and visual design were created from scratch during the jam period.

You are the overseer of a mysterious cult devoted to an ancient statue known as La Maestra. Manage your followers through their entire lifecycle - from innocent children to devoted adults, and finally to wise elders. Each stage of life holds different value in the eyes of the Godess.

Balance the needs of your cult between expansion and devotion. Gather resources, construct buildings, and grow your settlement. When the time comes, choose which followers to sacrifice at the altar, collecting their souls to fuel the statue's ascension. Upgrade La Maestra twice to complete your dark purpose and witness the culmination of your devotion.

Experience a haunting (but also a little bit cute) atmosphere where time itself bends around the statue. Watch as trees grow and wither, cultists age and die, and the cycle of life and death feeds an ever-hungry deity. Simple point-and-click controls let you command your followers, assign tasks, and make the hard choices that define a true cult leader.
Created in 48 hours for Unity 20th Anniversary Game Jam
All graphics, 3D models and music are original

[0.10.0] Post Jam Version [Planned date: 15.11.2025]
[Fix for:] Cultists not picking up resources when clicked[Fix for:] Cultists aborting building jobs in the middle of the process[Improvement/Fix for:] Not clear storage/resources info on statue, storage and buildings.[Fix for:] Skeletons hard to click [Fix for:] Skeletons spawned within the circle of influence are not interactable[Fix for:] Woodberry bushes regrowing too fast.[Fix for:] Cultists going to fixed location within building instead of to the closest position to them. [Improvement] When you order cultist to pick up a resource, now he picks up also other resources around.[Improvement] Buildings requirements (resources) take now into consideration also resources on the floor.   [Improvement] When building idle cultists will start building automatically.[Improvement] When building cultists will use also resources on the floor.[Improvement] Cultists continue on exploiting nearby resources of the same type (e.g. if they chop tree, they start to chop next one)[Improvement] Custom cursor.[Improvement] Descriptions when hovering over buttons and objects.[Improvement] You can click on resource and click 'work' button if you have any idle cultists - the closest one will automatically start working on the resource.


FOR BEST EXPERIENCE DOWNLOAD WINDOWS VERSION!

details.title: Stillshot
details.description:
Stillshot is a first-person puzzle-platformer where you halt time by taking snapshots.
 Take photos to freeze whatever’s in frame, such as parkour platforms or hazards.
Controls
 
  WASD – Move
   Shift (hold) – Sprint
   Space – Jump
   Right Click – Aim camera
   Left Click – Take snapshot (freeze what’s in frame)
   Tab – Open photo gallery (manage your stored shots)
  Esc - Pause / Retry / Level Select (General unstuck button)
The goal is to reach the door at the end of each level.

details.title: Veil of Rust
details.description:
Made for Unity 20th Anniversary Game Jam within 48 hours.
THEME: Timeless
This game follows in the footsteps of Timeless classics. The game also features a strict time limit.

You wake in a dim inn room beside a dead woman. The guards are coming for you. You have one minute to get out without getting caught.
Controls:
WASD for movement
TAB for inventory
E for interacting
I'm looking to expanding this game once the gamejam is over.

Also check out my current project: https://talehammer.itch.io/for-evelyn-2

game.short_text: TEXT-BASED SIMULATION ADVENTURE. LIVE BEYOND THE END OF CIVILISATION.
game.title: Till Nightfall

details.title: Till Nightfall
details.description:
uses the timeless genre of text based games to explore a fasinating story set in an apocalyptic world, where very few things prevail. Through your desicions, you are to sustain yourself, or employ your inevitable demise.

7:00 AM - No one, no water, no food, nothing.
9:00 AM - Reapiring the bunker, with few materials left
3:00 PM - Went out to the forest, in hopes of finding something to eat, and sustain ourselves till nightfall.
7:00 PM - The Dark follows the footsteps, the undead start rising.
9:00 PM - ...


DO NOT PANIC, KEEP CALM, AND MAKE WISE DECISIONS  OR ELSE THE NIGHT WILL FALL

moreInfo.Rating: Rated 5.0 out of 5 stars(1 total ratings)
moreInfo.Tags: Game Jam, Horror, Narrative, No AI, Pixel Art, Retro, Singleplayer, Unity, Zombies

details.title: Photo
details.description:
Time is something that never stops moving; it always travels forward and can never be reversed. I believe many people think so as well. In the same way, good memories also pass by. Capturing those memories through photography is like freezing that moment in time, allowing us to reminisce whenever we look back at it. No matter how much time passes, the memories within those photographs will never change.
Therefore, this game is about taking pictures of things to explore the main character's memories. May you all immerse yourselves in those memories along with those photographs.

moreInfo.Authors: Bas_ake, Zliplier, onJira, parichatrna198, xFallerz, PhuphaRaccoonz, RainWolf
moreInfo.Tags: 3D, Cozy, Isometric, Point & Click, Unity

details.title: The Timeless Innkeeper
details.description:
It’s a narrative-driven strategy game where you play as an innkeeper NPC, shaping heroes’ fates through limited dialogue cards, while balancing survival, income, and the innkeeper’s evolving humanity.
You can use cards to control hero and change the ending. 





#tip : you can get affection by giving hero right card
